content.title: Events System
content.description:
This page provides the complete API reference for Hobson's event system. For a conceptual overview, see [concept:event-system](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000).

---

## Overview

![](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000#description)

---

## API Reference

### `api.events.on(eventTypeId, handler)`

Subscribe to an event type. Returns unsubscribe function.

```javascript
const unsubscribe = api.events.on(api.EVENT_IDS.ITEM_UPDATED, (event) => {
  console.log('Updated:', event.content.item.name);
});

// Later:
unsubscribe();
```

### `api.events.off(eventTypeId, handler)`

Remove a specific handler.

### `api.events.emit(event)`

Emit an event. Typically only kernel code emits core events.

```javascript
api.events.emit({
  type: 'my-custom-event-uuid',
  content: { message: 'Hello!' }
});
```

### `api.events.list()`

Returns array of event type IDs with active listeners.

---

## Event Type Hierarchy

Event types form a type chain like items. Subscribing to a parent type receives all child events.

```
event-definition (base)                  e0e00000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
├── item-event                           e0e00000-0001-0000-0000-000000000000
│   ├── item:created                     e0e00000-0001-0001-0000-000000000000
│   ├── item:updated                     e0e00000-0001-0002-0000-000000000000
│   └── item:deleted                     e0e00000-0001-0003-0000-000000000000
└── system-event                         e0e00000-0002-0000-0000-000000000000
    ├── system:error                     e0e00000-0002-0001-0000-000000000000
    └── system:boot-complete             e0e00000-0002-0002-0000-000000000000
```

---

## Built-in Events

### Item Events

| Event | Constant | Content |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `item:created` | `ITEM_CREATED` | `{ id, item }` |
| `item:updated` | `ITEM_UPDATED` | `{ id, item, previous }` |
| `item:deleted` | `ITEM_DELETED` | `{ id, item }` |

### System Events

| Event | Constant | Content |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `system:error` | `SYSTEM_ERROR` | `{ error: {name, message, stack}, context }` |
| `system:boot-complete` | `SYSTEM_BOOT_COMPLETE` | `{ safeMode, debugMode, lateActivation }` |

---

## Declarative Watches

Code items can declare watches in `content.watches`. See [`declarativeWatches`](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=declarativeWatches) for implementation.

### Watch Declaration

```javascript
{
  type: api.IDS.LIBRARY,
  content: {
    watches: [
      { event: "e0e00000-0002-0002-0000-000000000000" },  // system:boot-complete
      { event: "e0e00000-0001-0002-0000-000000000000", type: "note-uuid" }  // item:updated for notes
    ],
    code: `
      export function onSystemBootComplete(content, api) {
        console.log('Library activated!');
      }
      
      export function onItemUpdated(content, api) {
        console.log('Note updated:', content.item.name);
      }
    `
  }
}
```

### Watch Filters

| Filter | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `event` | Event type GUID (required) |
| `type` | Match exact item type |
| `typeExtends` | Match type chain |
| `id` | Match specific item ID |

### Handler Naming

| Event | Handler |
|-------|--------|
| `item:created` | `onItemCreated` |
| `item:updated` | `onItemUpdated` |
| `item:deleted` | `onItemDeleted` |
| `system:error` | `onSystemError` |
| `system:boot-complete` | `onSystemBootComplete` |

---

## Error Handling

The kernel captures errors via [`captureError()`](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=errorCapture):

1. Error occurs
2. `captureError()` emits `system:error`
3. Handlers (like [default-error-handler](item://e7707000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002)) process it
4. If no handlers, kernel shows fallback banner

---

## Custom Event Types

```javascript
// Create custom event type
await api.set({
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: 'my-app:data-synced',
  type: api.EVENT_IDS.SYSTEM_EVENT,
  content: { description: 'Emitted when sync completes' }
});

// Rebuild cache to enable hierarchy dispatch
await kernel.buildEventTypeCache();
```

---

## Related

- [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) — EventBus implementation
- [default-error-handler](item://e7707000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) — Example event handler
